zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

title: Cross-reference comments documenting non-transfer are evidence, not slop

evidence.incident: ADR-T-001 (deterministic-calc separation, transferred to TaxBaseline Mini) and ADR-T-002 (multi-tenant isolation, deliberately NOT transferred) both contain explicit cross-references to wealth.com's parent ADR-001 + ADR-002 and use the parent's vocabulary (GRAT, OBBBA, §7520, multi-tenant) in *contrast tables* that say 'the parent had X; this does NOT'. The forbidden-token grep flagged these mentions.
evidence.naive_policy_outcome: If the policy were 'reject any match', the anti-overfit documentation itself would have to be removed — leaving readers unable to verify what the factory deliberately did NOT transfer. This would be self-defeating: the factory's anti-overfit credibility comes precisely from documenting non-transfer, not from pretending the parent run does not exist.

root_cause: The anti-overfit policy did not distinguish between 'forbidden token in product code' (true slop) and 'forbidden token in cross-reference / contrast documentation' (anti-overfit evidence).

skill_system_changes_applied.change: Added explicit anti-slop trigger distinguishing the three match categories (product-code leakage / cross-reference documentation / legitimate domain vocabulary). Rejection criterion is leakage in product code, not raw match count.
Step-4 classification rubric explicitly codifies the three categories with examples and actions (REJECT vs ACCEPT-with-annotation vs ACCEPT-with-allowlist-citation).

regression_check: Transfer-test acceptance records must include the three-category classification block; verdicts cannot be PASS while category-3 (product-code leakage) > 0, but raw match count > 0 in categories 1 and 2 does not block PASS. This is enforced in the transfer-test-acceptance-record schema and is now reviewable as part of df-quality-refinery's anti-slop triggers.
broader_principle: Documentation of negative space (what something is NOT) is a positive signal in governance work. Removing it weakens evidence. The dark-factory's culture of explicit anti-overfit cross-references is a feature, not a bug.
